texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Decision Support System (DSS) is the designated Managerial Cost Accounting (MCA) System of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) as mandated in the Veterans Health Administration (VHA) Directive 1750 VHA Managerial Cost Accounting System (Decision Support System (DSS), March 24, 2015.
texts.text: The Decision Support System (DSS) is the designated Managerial Cost Accounting (MCA) System of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) as mandated in the Veterans Health Administration (VHA) Directive 1750 VHA Managerial Cost Accounting System (Decision Support System (DSS), March 24, 2015.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DSS is a derived database built from standard VHA data sources. The Managerial Cost Accounting Office (MCAO) uses clinical and financial data to provide state-of-the-art activity-based costing and clinical productivity analyses.
texts.text: DSS is a derived database built from standard VHA data sources. The Managerial Cost Accounting Office (MCAO) uses clinical and financial data to provide state-of-the-art activity-based costing and clinical productivity analyses.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This is a design-to-schedule project with a compulsory Patch Release date of no later than November 1 of the new Fiscal Year (FY). This project enables the MCAO to accurately accommodate changes to the primary Clinical Transaction Systems made during the preceding year, ensuring the workload data has been accurately captured and costed to the Product Level.
texts.text: This is a design-to-schedule project with a compulsory Patch Release date of no later than November 1 of the new Fiscal Year (FY). This project enables the MCAO to accurately accommodate changes to the primary Clinical Transaction Systems made during the preceding year, ensuring the workload data has been accurately captured and costed to the Product Level.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: MCA Cost Data is used at all levels of the VA for functions such as budgeting and resource allocation. Additionally, the system contains a rich repository of clinical information used to promote a proactive approach to the care of high-risk (e.g., diabetes and acute coronary patients) and high-cost patients.
texts.text: MCA Cost Data is used at all levels of the VA for functions such as budgeting and resource allocation. Additionally, the system contains a rich repository of clinical information used to promote a proactive approach to the care of high-risk (e.g., diabetes and acute coronary patients) and high-cost patients.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The DSS Technical Manual serves the following purposes:
texts.text: The DSS Technical Manual serves the following purposes:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Provides technical information to aid Automated Data Processing Application Coordinators (ADPACs) and Office of Information and Technology (OIT) service staff responsible for implementing and maintaining the software.
texts.text: Provides technical information to aid Automated Data Processing Application Coordinators (ADPACs) and Office of Information and Technology (OIT) service staff responsible for implementing and maintaining the software.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Provides technical information to aid Database Administrator (DBA) personnel responsible for maintaining database files.
texts.text: Provides technical information to aid Database Administrator (DBA) personnel responsible for maintaining database files.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Provides security information for Information Security Officer (ISO) and Regional Information Security Officer (RISO) staff.
texts.text: Provides security information for Information Security Officer (ISO) and Regional Information Security Officer (RISO) staff.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The DSS Extracts Version 3.0 software provides a means of exporting data from selected Veterans Health Information Systems and Technology Architecture (VistA) software modules and transmitting it to a DSS database residing at the Austin Information Technology Center (AITC).
texts.text: The DSS Extracts Version 3.0 software provides a means of exporting data from selected Veterans Health Information Systems and Technology Architecture (VistA) software modules and transmitting it to a DSS database residing at the Austin Information Technology Center (AITC).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This transfer is accomplished through a set of extract routines, intermediate files, and transmission routines. Data from VistA packages is stored by the extract routines in the intermediate files, where it is temporarily available for local use and auditing. The data is then transmitted to the AITC, where it is formatted and uploaded into commercial software. After the data has been successfully uploaded into the commercial software, it is purged from the intermediate files.
texts.text: This transfer is accomplished through a set of extract routines, intermediate files, and transmission routines. Data from VistA packages is stored by the extract routines in the intermediate files, where it is temporarily available for local use and auditing. The data is then transmitted to the AITC, where it is formatted and uploaded into commercial software. After the data has been successfully uploaded into the commercial software, it is purged from the intermediate files.

It provides sufficient technical information about the software for developers and technical personnel to operate and maintain the software with only minimal assistance from Product Support staff.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There are no routine background jobs that must run to maintain normal package operation for DSS Extracts. However, when running a DSS data extract, users must execute it as a background task (queued through TaskManager).
texts.text: There are no routine background jobs that must run to maintain normal package operation for DSS Extracts. However, when running a DSS data extract, users must execute it as a background task (queued through TaskManager).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Both Statistical Analysis System (SAS) and Extract Audit reports can be displayed on the user’s screen, printed immediately to a print device, or queued to a print device. All other reports can only be printed as queued background jobs and cannot be stopped through the TaskMan User [XUTM USER] option.
texts.text: Both Statistical Analysis System (SAS) and Extract Audit reports can be displayed on the user’s screen, printed immediately to a print device, or queued to a print device. All other reports can only be printed as queued background jobs and cannot be stopped through the TaskMan User [XUTM USER] option.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.5 Special Note on IVP and UDP Extracts
texts.text: 3.5 Special Note on IVP and UDP Extracts

texts.label: text
texts.orig: IV orders will accumulate in the IV EXTRACT DATA file (#728.113); Unit Dose orders will accumulate in the UNIT DOSE EXTRACT DATA file (#728.904). These files are loaded dynamically as Pharmacy orders for IV and unit dose medicines are processed. Each record in these files uses approximately 100 bytes.
texts.text: IV orders will accumulate in the IV EXTRACT DATA file (#728.113); Unit Dose orders will accumulate in the UNIT DOSE EXTRACT DATA file (#728.904). These files are loaded dynamically as Pharmacy orders for IV and unit dose medicines are processed. Each record in these files uses approximately 100 bytes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Extreme caution must be used when performing maintenance on the IV EXTRACT DATA file (#728.113) and the UNIT DOSE EXTRACT DATA file (#728.904). If data in either file is purged, the IVP or UDP monthly extract may be incomplete. It is strongly recommended that, at a minimum, sites maintain all data from the current fiscal year.
texts.text: Extreme caution must be used when performing maintenance on the IV EXTRACT DATA file (#728.113) and the UNIT DOSE EXTRACT DATA file (#728.904). If data in either file is purged, the IVP or UDP monthly extract may be incomplete. It is strongly recommended that, at a minimum, sites maintain all data from the current fiscal year.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The holding files for IVP and UDP can be purged. Two functions were created to regenerate purged IVP and UDP holding file data. The steps shown in Table 10 must be executed from the Extract Manager’s Options menu to access these options.
texts.text: The holding files for IVP and UDP can be purged. Two functions were created to regenerate purged IVP and UDP holding file data. The steps shown in Table 10 must be executed from the Extract Manager’s Options menu to access these options.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Holding files can become quite large if appropriate purging is not performed. The option, Purge Extract Holding Files [ECXPURG], purges holding files from IVP and UDP by date range.
texts.text: Holding files can become quite large if appropriate purging is not performed. The option, Purge Extract Holding Files [ECXPURG], purges holding files from IVP and UDP by date range.

texts.text: This option analyzes the structure of a routine(s) to determine if the routine(s) adhere(s) to VistA Programming Standards. The XINDEX output includes the following components:

texts.orig: Compiled list of errors and warnings

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When the user executes XINDEX for a specified set of routines, deviations from VistA Programming Standards in the selected routine(s) and routine interactions (including routines called or called by other routines) are displayed.
texts.text: When the user executes XINDEX for a specified set of routines, deviations from VistA Programming Standards in the selected routine(s) and routine interactions (including routines called or called by other routines) are displayed.

texts.orig: To run XINDEX for the DSS software, specify the following namespaces at the "routine(s)? >" prompt:
texts.text: To run XINDEX for the DSS software, specify the following namespaces at the "routine(s)? >" prompt:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When XINDEX runs the DSS initialization routines residing in the User Class Identifier (UCI), compiled template routines and local routines found within the ECX namespace should be omitted at the "routine(s) ?>" prompt. To omit routines from selection, prefacing the namespace with a minus sign (-) is required.
texts.text: When XINDEX runs the DSS initialization routines residing in the User Class Identifier (UCI), compiled template routines and local routines found within the ECX namespace should be omitted at the "routine(s) ?>" prompt. To omit routines from selection, prefacing the namespace with a minus sign (-) is required.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This section provides an overview of the main menu and submenus available in the DSS application.  The user must hold the appropriate security keys in order to access all options.
texts.text: This section provides an overview of the main menu and submenus available in the DSS application.  The user must hold the appropriate security keys in order to access all options.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Security keys are assigned in VistA using the menu option
texts.text: Security keys are assigned in VistA using the menu option

texts.label: text
texts.orig: : Gives a user access to the ECX Management Menu.  This key should be allocated to the DSS manager or his/her designee to perform data extraction from VistA files and transmit this data to the commercial DSS database.
texts.text: : Gives a user access to the ECX Management Menu.  This key should be allocated to the DSS manager or his/her designee to perform data extraction from VistA files and transmit this data to the commercial DSS database.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: : Gives a DSS user access to the ‘Pharmacy Volume Edit and Edit Log’ option.
texts.text: : Gives a DSS user access to the ‘Pharmacy Volume Edit and Edit Log’ option.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: : Gives a DSS user access to the ECX FISCAL YEAR EXTRACT option.  This key should be given only to holders of the ECXMGR key whenever the site has been enrolled as an official DSS test site for the fiscal year conversion.  This key should be removed from the user(s) whenever the national released version of the DSS Fiscal Year patch is installed.
texts.text: : Gives a DSS user access to the ECX FISCAL YEAR EXTRACT option.  This key should be given only to holders of the ECXMGR key whenever the site has been enrolled as an official DSS test site for the fiscal year conversion.  This key should be removed from the user(s) whenever the national released version of the DSS Fiscal Year patch is installed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The DSS Extracts software contains one primary menu option, Extract Manager’s Options [ECXMGR], which contains options for the following six submenus. These options are fixed and are not subject to modification except by the software developers. These options are accessed by entering Extract Manager’s Options from the Systems Manager Menu; the menu options are listed in Table 14.
texts.text: The DSS Extracts software contains one primary menu option, Extract Manager’s Options [ECXMGR], which contains options for the following six submenus. These options are fixed and are not subject to modification except by the software developers. These options are accessed by entering Extract Manager’s Options from the Systems Manager Menu; the menu options are listed in Table 14.

texts.label: text

texts.text: Table 15  Steps to Obtain Information on Exported Menus

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DSS Extracts utilize several mail groups. The name of each mail group is prefaced with the letters “DSS”. There is a mail group for each type of data extract (e.g., DSS-BCM, DSS-ADMS, etc.) for the purpose of receiving messages when extracts are generated and data is transmitted to the AITC. DSS also utilizes mail groups DMS, DMV and DMU for the purpose of receiving confirmation messages from the AITC. DSS Extracts do not utilize alerts.
texts.text: DSS Extracts utilize several mail groups. The name of each mail group is prefaced with the letters “DSS”. There is a mail group for each type of data extract (e.g., DSS-BCM, DSS-ADMS, etc.) for the purpose of receiving messages when extracts are generated and data is transmitted to the AITC. DSS also utilizes mail groups DMS, DMV and DMU for the purpose of receiving confirmation messages from the AITC. DSS Extracts do not utilize alerts.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There is no special interfacing required for the VistA DSS Extracts software.
texts.text: There is no special interfacing required for the VistA DSS Extracts software.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This section lists the Integration Control Registrations (ICRs) created and/or used by DSS. They are categorized by the subscriber relationship to the software. No private ICRs are documented below.
texts.text: This section lists the Integration Control Registrations (ICRs) created and/or used by DSS. They are categorized by the subscriber relationship to the software. No private ICRs are documented below.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All the DSS Extracts options have been designed to be stand-alone options. Each option may be independently invoked.
texts.text: All the DSS Extracts options have been designed to be stand-alone options. Each option may be independently invoked.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The DSS Extracts software does not contain any package-wide variables that must be defined or are required for the package to run.
texts.text: The DSS Extracts software does not contain any package-wide variables that must be defined or are required for the package to run.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There are no additional security management related concerns associated with the DSS application.
texts.text: There are no additional security management related concerns associated with the DSS application.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The DSS Extracts software does not impose any additional legal requirements on the user, nor does it relieve the user of any legal requirements.
texts.text: The DSS Extracts software does not impose any additional legal requirements on the user, nor does it relieve the user of any legal requirements.

texts.text: Table 17 lists the recommended VA FileMan access codes for the DSS Extract Software.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The DSS Extracts software does not use electronic signatures.
texts.text: The DSS Extracts software does not use electronic signatures.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Data is transmitted to the AITC using VA MailMan mail messages. The first line of each message is a header that identifies the following entities: Site, Extract, Year and Month, SAS version identifier, and Fiscal Year Logic in the format shown in Figure 1.
texts.text: Data is transmitted to the AITC using VA MailMan mail messages. The first line of each message is a header that identifies the following entities: Site, Extract, Year and Month, SAS version identifier, and Fiscal Year Logic in the format shown in Figure 1.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DSS Extracts transmit messages containing extracted data to a specified queue located at the AITC. Data is transmitted via VA MailMan at the site’s discretion. Confirmation messages are received from the AITC indicating the extract messages were successfully transmitted.
texts.text: DSS Extracts transmit messages containing extracted data to a specified queue located at the AITC. Data is transmitted via VA MailMan at the site’s discretion. Confirmation messages are received from the AITC indicating the extract messages were successfully transmitted.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DSS Extracts have no archiving capability. There is an option to delete the local extract files after transmission to the commercial software. See section 10.1.
texts.text: DSS Extracts have no archiving capability. There is an option to delete the local extract files after transmission to the commercial software. See section 10.1.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DSS Extracts export the Delete Extract Files [ECXDELEF] option which can be used to delete individual extracts residing in files #727.802 through #727.833, or a range of extracts.
texts.text: DSS Extracts export the Delete Extract Files [ECXDELEF] option which can be used to delete individual extracts residing in files #727.802 through #727.833, or a range of extracts.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Data that resides in the holding files for VBECS (VBECS DSS EXTRACT file (#6002.03)), IVP (IV EXTRACT DATA file (#728.113)), and UDP (UNIT DOSE EXTRACT DATA file (#728.904)) extracts can be purged using the Purge Extract Holding Files [ECXPURG].
texts.text: Data that resides in the holding files for VBECS (VBECS DSS EXTRACT file (#6002.03)), IVP (IV EXTRACT DATA file (#728.113)), and UDP (UNIT DOSE EXTRACT DATA file (#728.904)) extracts can be purged using the Purge Extract Holding Files [ECXPURG].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Extreme caution should be exercised when purging or deleting data for the following reasons:
texts.text: Extreme caution should be exercised when purging or deleting data for the following reasons:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Any existing extract may be deleted (including transmitted and un-transmitted), as well as extracts that did not run to completion due to errors or system problems.
texts.text: Any existing extract may be deleted (including transmitted and un-transmitted), as well as extracts that did not run to completion due to errors or system problems.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Choosing a range of extracts or holding files sometimes results in an excessively large number of records to be deleted or purged, and may be very resource intensive. These tasks should be scheduled for non-peak hours, and the number of deleted or holding files purged should be limited to a single queued session.
texts.text: Choosing a range of extracts or holding files sometimes results in an excessively large number of records to be deleted or purged, and may be very resource intensive. These tasks should be scheduled for non-peak hours, and the number of deleted or holding files purged should be limited to a single queued session.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Extreme caution must be used when performing maintenance to the IV EXTRACT DATA file (#728.113) and the UNIT DOSE EXTRACT DATA file (#728.904). If data in either file is purged, the IVP or UDP monthly extract may be incomplete. It is strongly recommended that, at a minimum, sites maintain all data from the
texts.text: Extreme caution must be used when performing maintenance to the IV EXTRACT DATA file (#728.113) and the UNIT DOSE EXTRACT DATA file (#728.904). If data in either file is purged, the IVP or UDP monthly extract may be incomplete. It is strongly recommended that, at a minimum, sites maintain all data from the

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A user can only delete extracts that are associated with the division assigned in their NEW PERSON file (#200).
texts.text: A user can only delete extracts that are associated with the division assigned in their NEW PERSON file (#200).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Two functions were created to regenerate IVP and UDP holding file data that has been purged. These functions are located at:
texts.text: Two functions were created to regenerate IVP and UDP holding file data that has been purged. These functions are located at:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Extract Manager Options > Transmission Management > Recreate Extract Holding Files
texts.text: Extract Manager Options > Transmission Management > Recreate Extract Holding Files

texts.orig: The functions are listed in Table 18.
texts.text: The functions are listed in Table 18.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Table 18  Recreate IVP and UDP Holding File Options
texts.text: Table 18  Recreate IVP and UDP Holding File Options

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Purging of any local VistA extract data or VistA source of extract data (i.e., lab data, etc.) is not recommended until a facility has successfully created extracts, transmitted them to the AITC, audited the counts, loaded the data into DSS, and the validation results are successful.
texts.text: Purging of any local VistA extract data or VistA source of extract data (i.e., lab data, etc.) is not recommended until a facility has successfully created extracts, transmitted them to the AITC, audited the counts, loaded the data into DSS, and the validation results are successful.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The IV EXTRACT DATA file (#728.113) and UNIT DOSE EXTRACT DATA file (#728.904) can become quite large if appropriate purging is not performed. The file purge option, Purge Extract Holding Files [ECXPURG], removes the data from these files by date range. It is recommended that records over two fiscal years old should be purged from the IV EXTRACT DATA file (#728.113) and the UNIT DOSE EXTRACT DATA file (#728.904).
texts.text: The IV EXTRACT DATA file (#728.113) and UNIT DOSE EXTRACT DATA file (#728.904) can become quite large if appropriate purging is not performed. The file purge option, Purge Extract Holding Files [ECXPURG], removes the data from these files by date range. It is recommended that records over two fiscal years old should be purged from the IV EXTRACT DATA file (#728.113) and the UNIT DOSE EXTRACT DATA file (#728.904).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: VBECS holding files can also be purged. The VBECS holding file cannot be regenerated; therefore, extreme care must be used when purging these files.
texts.text: VBECS holding files can also be purged. The VBECS holding file cannot be regenerated; therefore, extreme care must be used when purging these files.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Technical users of the DSS software should ensure that a local contingency plan is used in the event of application problems in a live environment. The plan should identify the procedure(s) for maintaining the functionality provided by the DSS Extracts software in the event of a system outage. Field Station ISOs can get assistance from the Regional ISO.
texts.text: Technical users of the DSS software should ensure that a local contingency plan is used in the event of application problems in a live environment. The plan should identify the procedure(s) for maintaining the functionality provided by the DSS Extracts software in the event of a system outage. Field Station ISOs can get assistance from the Regional ISO.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Specific error correction guidance is provided in the ECX*3.0*196 DIBRG, which can be found in the VA Software Document Library (VDL).
texts.text: Specific error correction guidance is provided in the ECX*3.0*196 DIBRG, which can be found in the VA Software Document Library (VDL).

texts.text: 12.2 National Service Desk and Organizational Contacts

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Enterprise Service Desk (ESD) Provides critical IT support to Veterans Affairs.  Contact information can be found online at the VA YourIT service portal.
texts.text: The Enterprise Service Desk (ESD) Provides critical IT support to Veterans Affairs.  Contact information can be found online at the VA YourIT service portal.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This section describes some of the various methods for users to access DSS Technical documentation. Online DSS technical documentation pertaining to the DSS software, in addition to that which is located in the Help prompts and Online Help screens, are imbedded throughout the DSS package and can be accessed through utilization of several KERNEL options. These include, but are not limited to,
texts.text: This section describes some of the various methods for users to access DSS Technical documentation. Online DSS technical documentation pertaining to the DSS software, in addition to that which is located in the Help prompts and Online Help screens, are imbedded throughout the DSS package and can be accessed through utilization of several KERNEL options. These include, but are not limited to,

texts.text: XINDEX, Menu Management Inquire Option File, Print Option File,

texts.orig: Entering question marks at the "Select ... Option:" prompt provides users with valuable technical information. For example:
texts.text: Entering question marks at the "Select ... Option:" prompt provides users with valuable technical information. For example:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A single question mark (?) lists all options accessible from the current option.
texts.text: A single question mark (?) lists all options accessible from the current option.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Entering two question marks (??) lists all options accessible from the current option displaying the formal name and lock for each.
texts.text: Entering two question marks (??) lists all options accessible from the current option displaying the formal name and lock for each.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Three question marks (???) displays a brief description for each option in a menu.
texts.text: Three question marks (???) displays a brief description for each option in a menu.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: An option name preceded by a single question mark (?) displays extended Help, if available, for that option.
texts.text: An option name preceded by a single question mark (?) displays extended Help, if available, for that option.

table_cells.text:
TaskMan user option.
Users can stop the Extract Background Task while it is running by using the TaskMan user [XUTM USER] option.
If queued as a background task, users can stop the background task while it is running by using the TaskMan user [XUTM USER] option.

Delete Extract Files 
Data in any resulting incomplete extract should be deleted using the Delete Extract Files [ECXDELEF] option.

Purge Extract Holding Files.
Data in the holding files for the IVP, UDP and VBECS files can be purged using the Purge Extract Holding Files [ECXPURG] option.

table_cells.text: Logical Observation Identifiers, Names, and Codes
Veterans Health Information Systems and Technology Architecture

grid.text: Logical Observation Identifiers, Names, and Codes
Veterans Health Information Systems and Technology Architecture

table_cells.text: This system implements the VistA database engine and is the basis for several patient safety controls, as well as fiscal integrity controls. It implements security, confidentiality, and privacy controls, and is a critical component in meeting the requirements of Enterprise Architecture.

This system implements security, confidentiality, and privacy controls for VistA, including user authentication algorithms. It provides many tools for the safe construction of local software, and it implements many national control files, including, but not limited to, New Person, Institution, State, etc. This system is a critical component in meeting the requirements of Enterprise Architecture.

This system supports client and/or server messaging used by CPRS, Bar Code Medication Administration (BCMA), and others, to access the MUMPS database through APIs. It provides a development kit for local development, and it implements security, confidentiality, and privacy controls. This system is a critical component in meeting the requirements of Enterprise Architecture.
